name: ESP32-C3-DevKitM-1
description: Compact RISC-V development board built around the ESP32-C3-MINI-1 module, with two 15-pin headers, USB-to-UART bridge, BOOT/RST buttons and an addressable RGB LED.
notes: On-board addressable RGB LED (WS2812-style) on GPIO8.
GPIO11-17 are used by the in-package SPI flash and are not broken out on the headers.
ADC2 (GPIO5) is unusable while Wi-Fi is active and is deprecated in ESP-IDF; prefer ADC1 (GPIO0-4).
All GPIOs support PWM via the LEDC peripheral; all GPIOs are 3.3 V only.
Header order taken from the J1/J3 pin layout tables in the official Espressif user guide; J1 is the left column, J3 the right column, viewed from above with USB at the bottom.

pins.label: IO2
pins.caps: DIGITAL_IN | DIGITAL_OUT | PWM | ADC | RTC | STRAPPING
pins.warning: Strapping pin; must be high at reset in both normal and download boot modes

pins.label: TX
pins.warning: UART0 TX; used by the default console and the USB-to-UART flashing bridge

pins.label: RX
pins.warning: UART0 RX; used by the default console and the USB-to-UART flashing bridge

pins.label: IO9
pins.warning: BOOT strapping pin (internal pull-up); must be high at reset to boot from flash, low enters download mode

pins.label: IO8
pins.warning: Strapping pin; must be high when entering download mode (GPIO9 low); also drives the on-board RGB LED

pins.label: IO5
pins.warning: ADC2 channel; unusable while Wi-Fi is active and deprecated in ESP-IDF

pins.label: IO18
pins.warning: USB Serial/JTAG D-; using it as GPIO breaks the built-in USB connection

pins.label: IO19
pins.warning: USB Serial/JTAG D+; using it as GPIO breaks the built-in USB connection
